title: RecurrentGPT: Interactive Generation of (Arbitrarily) Long Text
key_claim: Simulates LSTM-style recurrence in natural language: short-term memory (10-20 sentences) plus long-term memory (paragraph summaries on disk + semantic retrieval) drives paragraph-by-paragraph generation with explicit per-step plan; treats prose generation as a step function with persistent state.
relevance: Foundational architecture for any 'continuously running creative agent' — confirms language-as-state pattern works for long-form fiction. Directly applicable to canon-state design.

title: DOC: Improving Long Story Coherence With Detailed Outline Control
key_claim: Two-stage system: a 'detailed outliner' produces a hierarchical, fine-grained plan; a 'detailed controller' constrains generation to follow that outline — yields +22.5% plot coherence, +28.2% outline relevance, +20.7% interestingness vs Re3 baseline; humans rated DOC much more controllable in interactive mode.
relevance: Demonstrates that hierarchical, controllable outlines are the SOTA-correct alternative to direct generation. Strong precedent for 'anchors + lens + branch promotion' interaction.

title: Re3: Generating Longer Stories With Recursive Reprompting and Revision
key_claim: Plan-Draft-Rewrite-Edit loop using a base LM and rerankers; +14% coherence and +20% premise-relevance over direct generation for 2k-word stories. Establishes the four-phase pipeline still inherited by 2025 multi-agent systems.
relevance: Direct ancestor of every 'multi-agent novel writer' on GitHub. Useful as a baseline architecture to beat.

title: Dramatron / Co-Writing Screenplays and Theatre Scripts with Language Models
key_claim: Hierarchical prompt-chaining (Chinchilla 70B): log line -> title/characters -> plot -> location descriptions -> dialogue. CHI'23 evaluation with 15 industry pros: 84% found it useful, 77% reported true collaboration. Open-sourced colab + repo.
relevance: Earliest credible 'serious creative agent' from a major lab; the hierarchical decomposition is canonical. Limited to ~screenplay-length output.

title: PlotMachines: Outline-Conditioned Generation with Dynamic Plot State Tracking
key_claim: EMNLP'20: introduces dynamic plot-state tracking conditioned on outline phrases; pre-LLM era but established that explicit state is needed because GPT-2/Grover alone produce incoherent narratives.
relevance: Historical baseline; informs why pure long-context generation is insufficient even today.

title: STORIUM: Dataset and Evaluation Platform for Machine-in-the-Loop Story Generation
key_claim: 6,000 collaborative stories (~125M tokens) with character goals/attributes annotated; live evaluation platform where authors query the model and edit suggestions, with edit-distance metrics correlating with quality.
relevance: The only public dataset that captures multi-author, persistent-character collaboration semantics — the closest existing analogue to author + creative-agent collaboration.

title: TaleBrush: Sketching Stories with Generative Pretrained Language Models
key_claim: CHI'22: line-sketch UI (drag a curve = protagonist fortune over time) controls a GPT-based story generator. Users iteratively sketch and read; control vs novelty trade-off measured.
relevance: Strongest existing prior art for 'author intervenes via lens, not prose' — non-typed control surface for narrative shape.

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (Park et al, Stanford/Google 2023)
key_claim: 25 LLM agents in Smallville with observation/planning/reflection memory loop produce believable autonomous behavior; one seed (Valentine's Day party) propagates through the social graph over two simulated days. Open-source 'reverie' framework.
relevance: Definitive prior art for 'always-running world simulator around the protagonist's contact graph' — directly applicable. Memory architecture (raw stream + reflections + retrieval) is canonical.

title: Project Sid: Many-Agent Simulations Toward AI Civilization (Altera, 2024)
key_claim: 10-1000+ agents in Minecraft with PIANO architecture (parallel information aggregation, multiple concurrent stateless modules sharing an Agent State); emergent specialization, rule-making, religion (Pastafarianism), and cultural memes. $9M seed.
relevance: Demonstrates scale: 1000+ persistent agents with concurrent cognition+action is feasible. Useful upper bound on what 'persistent contact graph around protagonist' could achieve.

title: Agents' Room: Narrative Generation through Multi-step Collaboration (DeepMind, ICLR 2025)
key_claim: Specialized planning agents + writing agents (one per narrative section: exposition/rising/climax/falling/resolution); produces stories ~2x longer than end-to-end baselines. Introduces 'Tell Me A Story' dataset.
relevance: DeepMind's official endorsement of multi-agent specialization for fiction; closest peer-reviewed precedent for the proposed architecture.

title: StoryWriter: A Multi-Agent Framework for Long Story Generation (CIKM 2025)
key_claim: Three-agent pipeline: outline agent (event-graph with character/event-event relations), planning agent (chapter-level event scheduling), writing agent (dynamic history compression). Generated 6,000 long stories (avg 8k words) and trained StoryWriterLLAMA / StoryWriterGLM via SFT.
relevance: Open-source, reproducible multi-agent baseline that matches several requirements (event graph, chapter planner). Strong starting point if rolling-your-own.

title: StoryBox: Collaborative Multi-Agent Simulation for Hybrid Bottom-Up Long-Form Story Generation (AAAI 2026)
key_claim: Combines top-down storyteller agent with bottom-up multi-agent sandbox (World/Region/Zone/Area/Object 5-level hierarchy); produces 12k-word stories that beat all baselines on coherence, creativity, character development. Reference site: storyboxproject.github.io.
relevance: Closest published analogue to 'always-running world sim that drafts chapters as canon stages settle' — the hybrid simulation+narration approach is exactly what this project envisions.

title: Long Story Generation via Knowledge Graph and Literary Theory (arXiv 2025)
key_claim: Multi-agent generator augmented with: (a) memory storage to prevent theme drift, (b) literary-theory-derived 'story theme obstacle' framework, (c) incremental knowledge graph for similarity computation, (d) writer-reader dialogue loop for revision. Beats prior outline-based methods.
relevance: Closest precedent for using a structured prior (here: narratology) on top of KG. The architectural slot for narratology generalizes cleanly to Bazi/Qimen as the prior.

title: BaZi-Based Character Simulation Benchmark (arXiv 2510.23337, 2025)
key_claim: First QA dataset for BaZi-based persona reasoning across wealth/health/kinship/career/relationships. Symbolic-LLM hybrid (BaZi rule analysis -> reasoning -> scenario interpretation) achieves 30.3-62.6% accuracy lift over DeepSeek-v3 / GPT-5-mini. Wrong BaZi data drops accuracy 20-45% — proving it's load-bearing, not flavor.
relevance: Direct evidence that Bazi-as-prior measurably improves character simulation. Validates the 'first-class probabilistic prior, not flavor text' design choice.

title: Bazi MCP Server for LLM agents
key_claim: Deterministic Bazi calculator exposed via MCP, handling DST and True Solar Time; ensures the LLM never hallucinates the chart, only interprets it. Designed for lifestyle apps, cultural platforms, and games.
relevance: Production-ready calculation layer; can be reused as the deterministic side of the BaZi/Qimen prior. Avoids reinventing the chart engine.

title: WebNovelBench: Placing LLM Novelists on the Web Novel Distribution
key_claim: 4,000+ Chinese web novels; eight narrative-quality dimensions; LLM-as-Judge plus PCA aggregation. Benchmarks 24 SOTA LLMs and ranks them against human-written masterpieces, popular web novels, and slop.
relevance: Only Chinese-web-novel-aware benchmark. Critical for evaluating xianxia output objectively.

title: Lost in Stories: Consistency Bugs in Long Story Generation by LLMs (2026)
key_claim: ConStory-Bench: 2,000 prompts, 4 task types, 5 error categories with 19 subtypes. Findings: consistency errors are most common in factual/temporal dimensions, cluster around the middle of narratives, occur in high-entropy segments, and co-occur. Releases ConStory-Checker for automated detection.
relevance: Empirical motivation for structured canon + verification gates. Tells us where errors cluster (mid-story, high-entropy) so the canon-gate UX can prioritize those zones.

title: BooookScore: A Systematic Exploration of Book-Length Summarization (ICLR 2024)
key_claim: Compares hierarchical merging vs incremental updating for >100k-token book summaries. Hierarchical merging gives more coherent summaries but loses detail; larger chunks improve incremental updates.
relevance: Directly informs the design of the persistent canon/memory book layer for long-running creative agents.

title: NexusSum: Hierarchical LLM Agents for Long-Form Narrative Summarization (ACL 2025)
key_claim: Multi-agent hierarchical chunking pipeline; up to +30% BERTScore over prior SOTA on book-length narrative summarization with scalable length control.
relevance: State-of-the-art recipe for the 'compress canon into agent-readable form' subroutine.

title: Weaver: Foundation Models for Creative Writing (arXiv 2401.17268, 2024)
key_claim: Mini/Base/Pro/Ultra (1.8B-34B) family pre-trained on a writing-focused corpus and aligned to professional writers; Chinese:English = 4:1 in training data; Weaver Ultra beats GPT-4 and GLM-4 on writing style/creativity by their internal benchmark.
relevance: Most credible Chinese-aware creative-writing foundation model; serious option for the prose-generation backbone.

title: Sudowrite Story Engine 3.0 (commercial)
key_claim: Braindump -> Genre/Style/Characters -> Outline -> Beats -> Draft. Generates 3,000-5,000+ word chapters from beats. Per current 2025-2026 reviews: 'fundamentally a scene-level tool you stitch into a book'; not a continuous pipeline.
relevance: Most mature commercial fiction tool. Confirms the gap: even SOTA commercial product is on-demand scene-by-scene, not continuous.

title: Novelcrafter (commercial; Codex + Plan + Matrix)
key_claim: Codex (typed entries with aliases) attaches to scene-beat completions; matrix view sorts scenes by codex entry; Extract tool turns chat outputs into Codex/scene-summary entries. Drag-drop acts/chapters/scenes. Bring-your-own-LLM.
relevance: Best 'world-bible aware' commercial workflow. The Codex pattern is reusable, but it's still author-typed and on-demand.

title: NovelAI (commercial)
key_claim: Llama 3 Erato 70B + Lorebook (typed memory); subscription $10-25/mo; positioned for free-form prose, anime imagery, and unfiltered content. Still turn-by-turn assistant, no autonomous loop.
relevance: Strongest 'unfiltered' option for cultivation/xianxia content; weakest on narrative structure.

title: Wavemaker (open source, free)
key_claim: Free, cross-platform PWA for novel planning (snowflake method, storyboard cards, etc). Open-source v2-v4. Not AI-first.
relevance: Reference for high-quality non-AI planning UX; useful for the planning/visualization side of the stack.

title: Plot Bunni (open source)
key_claim: Open-source novel-writing tool with AI brainstorming + drafting; works in browser on desktop/mobile.
relevance: Lower-friction OSS alternative; not multi-agent.

title: Squibler (commercial)
key_claim: Templates + Smart Writer + 2025 'Book Proposal' phase (genre/tone/POV/key points before drafting). Generally noted as repetitive vs Sudowrite for fiction.
relevance: Comparison point; not state-of-the-art for fiction.

title: SillyTavern (open source roleplay frontend)
key_claim: Character cards (.png+JSON), Lorebook, Auto-Continue (empty user message forces continuation), bring-your-own-backend. Best-in-class character-card ecosystem.
relevance: Closest community-tested precedent for 'persistent character + lore' chat; limited as novel writer because output is dialog-flavored.

title: AutoNovel by Nous Research (open source)
key_claim: Inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch loop. Modify-evaluate-keep/discard with explicit propagation debts in state.json (lore<->outline<->chapter); LLM Judge + mechanical regex anti-slop checks. First novel produced: 'The Second Son of the House of Bells' (19 chapters, 79,456 words).
relevance: The single most architecturally relevant open-source autonomous novel pipeline available in 2026. Anti-slop checks + propagation-debt model are reusable. English-centric.

title: AI_NovelGenerator (YILING0013, GitHub, Chinese)
key_claim: Chinese-first multi-chapter generator with explicit foreshadowing tracking (伏笔), character-arc state, semantic retrieval for long-range consistency, and contradiction-detection proofreading. Configurable LLM backend.
relevance: Best-in-class Chinese-aware open-source novel pipeline. Treats 伏笔 as a first-class entity — directly relevant to xianxia where seeded foreshadowing is genre-critical.

title: InkOS (open source) and AuthorClaw (open source)
key_claim: InkOS: local web workbench, AI-detection, style analysis, human review gates. AuthorClaw: 'security-hardened' end-to-end fiction pipeline targeting KDP-ready manuscripts.
relevance: Both inherit from AutoNovel-style pipelines. InkOS's 'review gate' aligns with the canon-gate UX; AuthorClaw's commercial framing is less relevant.

title: AI Cultivation World Simulator (Steam + GitHub)
key_claim: Open-world LLM-driven xianxia sim. Each NPC has its own LLM-backed personality, memory, goals; cultivation rules (spiritual roots, realms, techniques, lifespan, elixirs, spirit stones) are codified as constraints. BYOK (DeepSeek/Ollama/OpenAI compat).
relevance: Closest existing implementation of 'LLM agents + cultivation rules as a coherent world model' — useful reference even though it's a game, not a writing tool.

title: Cultivation World Simulator (4thfever / AI-Cultivation, GitHub)
key_claim: Open-source agentic-workflow xianxia world simulator, dynamic emergent world, configurable.
relevance: Codebase that already encodes xianxia-specific entities (sect, lifespan, techniques) — reusable as a starter for the world-state side.

title: AutoGPT / BabyAGI / Auto-GPT critiques
key_claim: Recursive task loops 'distract' on tangents (the 'shoelace' anecdote); confused by their own loops; 'autonomy' is shallow next-token prediction in a wrapper. Fiction-specific creative writing experiments existed but produced incoherent novel-length output.
relevance: Cautionary baseline; argues why naive AutoGPT-style loops fail for fiction without strong canon/state and review gates.

title: Character.AI: PipSqueak 2 (PSQ2) + Lorebook (April 2026)
key_claim: Two-layer memory: short-term context + RAG/embedding long-term store; Pinned Memories + Memory Box; PSQ2 rolled out April 2026 to c.ai+, broader May 2026. Still session-biased, prone to drift.
relevance: Confirms 'two-layer memory' is now industry-standard, but mainstream consumer apps still under-invest in true canon. We need to do better than this for novel-grade output.

title: Gemini 3.1 Ultra (2M context) and Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M beta)
key_claim: Gemini 3.1 Ultra ships 2M-token context (April 2026); Claude Sonnet 4.5 has a 1M beta via context-1m-2025-08-07 header. Both rated as best-in-class for coherent recall at 1M tokens, though structured retrieval still beats pure long-context for novel-length.
relevance: Foundation: enough context now exists to keep an entire novel + extensive canon in-frame. This makes the proposed architecture practically feasible.

title: Learning to Reason for Long-Form Story Generation (arXiv 2503.22828, 2025)
key_claim: Adapts RL-with-reasoning to long-form story generation despite the absence of verifiable rewards / labeled completions; first serious attempt to bring R1-style reasoning to fiction.
relevance: Relevant to 'agent that pauses for high-risk branches' — gives a recipe for an LLM that reasons explicitly about choices.

title: Can LLMs Generate Good Stories? Insights and Challenges from a Narrative Planning Perspective (arXiv 2506.10161)
key_claim: Benchmark using Answer-Set Programming for causal-soundness/character-intentionality/dramatic-conflict. Finding: GPT-4-tier models are causally sound at small scales but fail at planning with intentional characters and dramatic conflict; argues symbolic narrative planning still has a role.
relevance: Empirical justification for keeping symbolic / structured planning (incl. Bazi/Qimen) alongside LLM generation. A pure-LLM pipeline cannot deliver the cultivation-novel beat structure.

title: SCORE: Story Coherence and Retrieval Enhancement for AI Narratives (arXiv 2503.23512)
key_claim: Incremental knowledge-graph construction for persistent story memory; multi-LLM-backend compatible; explainable industrial-scale narrative pipeline.
relevance: KG-as-canon design. Strong fit for xianxia where lineage/sect/realm relations are graph-structured.

title: Guiding Generative Storytelling with Knowledge Graphs (arXiv 2505.24803)
key_claim: Editable KGs coupled with LLM generation; user-driven updates; Graph-RAG argued superior to vector RAG for long fiction continuity.
relevance: Justification for KG backbone over pure vector RAG — directly applicable to canon design.

title: Yuewen Group / WriteWise / DeepSeek for Chinese web novels (industry)
key_claim: Yuewen has its own web-fiction-tuned LLM. WriteWise (Himalaya) for full-process authoring. DeepSeek widely used to draft 10,000-character chapters in ~1 hour for 起点 short-form satisfying novels (爽文). 38.7% of contracted Qidian works in 2025 are fantasy/xianxia.
relevance: The Chinese-market reality: AI-drafted xianxia is already a commercial activity. Most authors use it for 'first draft fast', not for continuous autonomy.

title: Building a 'Human-in-the-Loop' Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
key_claim: Approval gates as 'predefined moments' where a human validates before continuing; non-blocking parallel-feedback patterns; UX rule: don't dump raw JSON, summarize.
relevance: Direct UX guidance for the 'pauses for author decisions on high-risk branches' design.

title: From Pen to Prompt: How Creative Writers Integrate AI (CHI Creativity 2025)
key_claim: Interviewed 18 creative writers; they make deliberate decisions about *when* to invoke AI based on craft values (authenticity, voice). They reject 'AI does it all' workflows.
relevance: Author psychology: even for power users, 'always-running creative agent' must respect intentional intervention. Validates the 'anchors/lens, not prose' design.

title: PersonaGym: Evaluating Persona Agents and LLMs (Findings of EMNLP 2025)
key_claim: Benchmark for persistent persona consistency in LLMs across multi-turn settings; LLMs exhibit measurable persona drift over long interactions.
relevance: Quantifies the 'persona drift' problem that any always-running NPC/agent will hit. Argues for explicit persona/Bazi anchoring.

title: CollabStory: Multi-LLM Collaborative Story Generation and Authorship Analysis (NAACL 2025)
key_claim: Up to 5 LLM agents pass a story segment-by-segment; analyses authorship attribution across segments; finds strict turn-passing hurts coherence vs shared-canon designs.
relevance: Confirms that naive 'crowd-of-authors' degrades coherence; you need a shared canon, not just relay.

title: Multi-Agent Based Character Simulation for Story Writing (In2Writing 2025)
key_claim: Director Agent + Character Agents respond chronologically along a scene outline. Targets 1k-2k token scene generation. Models a 'writers' room'.
relevance: Lightweight, modular precedent for scene-level role-play that could plug into a continuous pipeline.

title: LongStory: Coherent, Complete and Length Controlled Long Story Generation (PAKDD 2024)
key_claim: Adds long/short-term context weight calibrator + structural-position discourse tokens; outperforms PlotMachines on coherence/completeness/relevance up to 10k tokens.
relevance: Plug-in idea: explicit position tokens for structural state could be reused in a xianxia 'realm-stage' position embedding.

title: StoryDiffusion (NeurIPS 2024) and StoryWeaver (AAAI 2025)
key_claim: Consistent self-attention across long image sequences for character-consistent multi-panel/video storytelling.
relevance: Adjacent — for cover art / illustration consistency. Not directly relevant to text canon but useful for ancillary product.

title: Awesome-Story-Generation (curated GitHub list)
key_claim: Most up-to-date public index of LLM-era story generation papers; useful as a watchlist.
relevance: Single best entry point to track ongoing literature. Recommended for future review cycles.

title: A Survey on LLMs for Story Generation (Findings of EMNLP 2025)
key_claim: Splits the field into (i) independent LLM generation and (ii) author-assistance / collaboration. The 'always-running creative agent' sits *between* the two and is under-explored.
relevance: Definitive 2025 academic taxonomy. Lets us position the proposed system precisely as a hybrid that the survey identifies as a gap.

title: Narrative Theory-Driven LLM Methods for Automatic Story Generation and Understanding: A Survey (arXiv 2602.15851, 2026)
key_claim: 2026 survey arguing narrative theory (Aristotelian, Freytag, Propp, etc.) is increasingly applicable to LLM extraction/classification/generation without large annotated datasets.
relevance: Generalizes the 'theory-as-prior' design choice. Bazi/Qimen fits as a non-Western variant of narrative theory in the same architectural slot.

title: 6tail/lunar-javascript
key_claim: Reference-grade Chinese lunar calendar library covering solar terms, gan-zhi, four pillars (bazi), nayin, ten gods, shensha, taboo days, lunar mansions; MIT license; v1.7.4 published May 2026 on cdnjs.
relevance: Primary backend candidate for deterministic bazi pillar generation. Sister Python lib (lunar-python) makes it cross-stack.
reliability: High. Most-cited Chinese lunar lib in Asian dev community; powers iztro, qfdk/qimen, ziwei-doushu engines.

title: yuangu/sxtwl_cpp (Shouxing astronomical calendar)
key_claim: C++ port of Shouxing astronomical calendar with verified data BC722-1960 against Zhang Peiyu, Chen Yuan, Fang Shiming historical tables. Python/Java/Lua bindings via SWIG; long double precision.
relevance: Authoritative gold standard for date->ganzhi conversion when historical accuracy matters; useful as ground-truth oracle for testing lunar-javascript.
reliability: High. Calibrated against three independent historical reference works.

title: alvamind/bazi-calculator-by-alvamind
key_claim: Modern TypeScript bazi calculator and analyzer for Node.js with full pillar + analysis API. npm/yarn/pnpm install.
relevance: Higher-level wrapper alternative to building on raw lunar-javascript; designed for application embedding.
reliability: Medium. Single-author repo; less battle-tested than lunar-javascript.

title: cantian-ai/bazi-mcp
key_claim: MCP server (TypeScript, ISC, Node 22+) exposing getBaziDetail, getSolarTimes, getChineseCalendar. Originated as the OpenAI GPT Store 'Chinese Bazi Fortune Teller' product. Underlying calc lib not specified in docs; school not declared.
relevance: Direct precedent for invoking bazi tools from an LLM agent (our exact use case). MCP transport already aligns with our Hermes stack.
reliability: Medium-high. Actively maintained, productized; opaque on calc internals.

title: BaZi-Based Character Simulation Benchmark (arxiv 2510.23337)
key_claim: First benchmark for bazi persona reasoning. Celebrity-50 dataset (50 people, 488 QA pairs across wealth/health/kinship/career/relationships). BaZi-LLM hybrid (rule-based mapper + DeepSeek-R1 generator + Doubao-1.5-Thinking reasoner) beats DeepSeek-v3 by 30.3%, GPT-5-mini by 62.6%. Shuffled-birthdays ablation drops accuracy by up to 45.7% confirming temporal alignment. Achieves 60% on 2024 Global Fortune-Teller Championship (3rd place tier).
relevance: Strongest empirical evidence that symbolic-bazi-as-prior actually improves LLM persona/temporal reasoning. Directly validates our thesis.
reliability: High for direction; medium for absolute numbers (LLM-generated narratives in dataset bias risk acknowledged by authors).

title: MirrorAI-Lab/BaZi-Persona
key_claim: Companion repo to arxiv 2510.23337 paper. Hosts Celebrity50_zh.json plus the rule-based mapping + LLM glue. 13 stars, 4 commits.
relevance: Reference implementation for bazi-as-prior in narrative; small enough to cherry-pick the rule mapper.

title: DestinyLinker/MingLi-Bench
key_claim: MIT-licensed Python benchmark; 160 multiple-choice questions across 12 life dimensions sourced from 2022-2025 Global Fortune Teller Competition. Pre-computes bazi/ziwei via iztro, supports openai/anthropic/google/deepseek/doubao via --astro and --cot flags. Tianfu Agent reports 50% trimmed-mean accuracy vs 40% best-LLM baseline (human top-20 average 53.5%).
relevance: Externally validated evaluation harness we can adopt verbatim for our own bazi-aware narrative branch scorer.
reliability: High. Reproducible CLI, transparent scoring, multi-vendor.

title: FANzR-arch/Numerologist_skills
key_claim: Engineering framework specifically targeting LLM hallucinations in Chinese astrology. Three-layer architecture: (1) Python deterministic computation (ships qimen_cli.py for actual qimen 排盘), (2) reference-document rule constraints, (3) prompt-engineering for inquiry order. Covers qimen + ziwei + bazi.
relevance: Closest to the architecture we should adopt: deterministic core + reference-rule layer + LLM expression. Validates 'don't ask the LLM to do the math' principle.
reliability: Medium. Solo-author but architecturally aligned with industry practice.

title: qfdk/qimen
key_claim: MIT Node.js qimen library; 转盘排法 (rotating-disk method). Built on Express + lunar-javascript. Modular files: bamen.js (eight gates), bashen.js (eight spirits), jiuxing.js (nine stars), dipan.js (earth disk). Outputs nine-palace grid with heaven disk, eight gates, nine stars, eight spirits, hidden stems.
relevance: Drop-in qimen 转盘 backend. Modular file split makes it easy to swap school logic.
reliability: Medium. Deterministic logic but limited automated tests visible.

title: dxbuyi/qimen.skill (Claude Code skill)
key_claim: MIT-licensed Claude Code skill: Python (qimen_paipan.py) does deterministic 时家奇门 转盘 排盘, emits both human nine-palace grid and JSON between markers. Claude is restricted to interpretation (用神 → 格局 → 门星神 → 生克 → conclusion). Authors flag ~1-day error margins near solar-term transitions.
relevance: Direct architectural template for our Novel System: math in deterministic Python, narrative in LLM. Same separation-of-concerns we want.
reliability: High for design intent; medium for calc precision (self-acknowledged 1-day boundary errors).

title: oceanjustinlin/qimen
key_claim: MIT JS/Vue/Python project. Two-tier AI: Gemini-Flash router classifies the question into 6 domains (财运/事业/感情/健康/交易/杂事), Gemini-Pro produces deep analysis. Daily/monthly/annual scoring 45-98 with deterministic local calc. Bazi engine handles solar/lunar/ganzhi input + true-solar-time correction + day-master strength + favorable elements + 三柱 timeline.
relevance: Demonstrates the question-routing pattern (intent classification before symbolic interpretation) we'll need for narrative branches mapped to event-types.
reliability: Medium. Active personal project, polished UI.

title: SylarLong/iztro
key_claim: MIT TypeScript ziwei doushu library, 3.7k stars, ~20KB minified. Generates 12-palace charts, four pillars, major/minor luck cycles + transits, four-transformations detection. ES6/CJS/CDN; supports zh-CN/zh-TW/en/ja/ko/vi. v2.3+ offers plugin system for school variants.
relevance: Best-in-class ziwei backend if we add 紫微 as a parallel layer to bazi. Plugin system aligns with school-pluggable architecture.
reliability: High. Most popular ziwei JS lib; adopted by MingLi-Bench and Renhuai123/ziwei-doushu.

title: masterai-top/Bazi-Ziwei-Qimen-Dunjia-Divination-System-Source-Code
key_claim: Java commercial-grade source covering bazi + ziwei + qimen + 七政四余 + 大六壬 + 神煞. Web realtime 排盘.
relevance: Reference implementation for cross-system normalization (multiple Chinese metaphysics schools simultaneously).
reliability: Medium. Marketed for commercial license; quality of inner algorithms not auditable from README alone.

title: Zhihu - 八字流派简介 (Bazi schools overview)
key_claim: Standard Chinese-language reference describing 子平派 (rule-based, Ten Gods, ancient corpus 渊海子平 + 三命通会), 盲派 (image-driven, event-prediction-focused, oral lineage), 新派 (modern numeric day-master strength).
relevance: Confirms the three-school taxonomy we need to choose between. 子平派 is the canonical school for personality archetype mapping.
reliability: Medium. Community blog; aligns with multiple independent sources.

title: Easy Feng Shui - Qimen 转盘 vs 飞盘
key_claim: 转盘 (rotating-disk) - symbols/gates/stars rotate as a whole, 八神, faster but ~60% accuracy claim; 飞盘 (flying-palace) - symbols arranged sequentially yang-up/yin-down, 九神, slower but ~80-90% accuracy claim. 转盘 = 后天 relationships; 飞盘 = 先天.
relevance: Direct decision-input for which qimen variant to standardize on. Most open-source libs implement 转盘 (qfdk, dxbuyi); we should match for ecosystem alignment unless higher precision is required.
reliability: Medium. Practitioner accuracy claims unverified; structural distinction is canonical.

title: Dougles Chan - The Different Schools of Qi Men Dun Jia
key_claim: Four scope schools - 年家/月家/日家/时家奇门. Modern practice converges on 时家奇门 for resolution. 时家 splits into 拆补法 (Chai Bu) and 置闰法 (Zhi Run). Year-stem method now archaic.
relevance: Tells us which qimen scope to use for narrative-branch decisions: 时家 (hour-level) - that maps cleanly to in-story event timing.
reliability: Medium. Practitioner authority but commercially incentivized.

title: Qimen Dunjia - Wikipedia
key_claim: Encyclopedic background on three奇 (乙丙丁), 六仪 (戊己庚辛壬癸), 八门 (休生伤杜景死惊开), 九星, 八神/九神, 九宫. Historically a strategic/military divination tool (Zhuge Liang associated). Used for picking action timing and direction.
relevance: Definitive vocabulary mapping we need for symbol -> narrative-event semantics.
reliability: High for general structure; thin on contemporary algorithmic details.

title: Cultist Simulator (Weather Factory / Alexis Kennedy)
key_claim: Card-based occult narrative sim (2018). Aspect cycle Lantern -> Forge -> Edge -> Winter -> Heart -> Grail -> Moth -> ... fixed compass of metaphysical principles that drive every card and ritual. 7 years of design behind a 'feels-like-magic' opacity model.
relevance: Closest design analogue to bazi-as-prior: a small fixed set of symbolic principles + combinatorial narrative engine. Validates that opaque-by-design symbolic systems work commercially.

title: CK3 Dev Diary #58 - Stre(ss)tching the traits
key_claim: Crusader Kings 3 stress system: each character has 3 personality traits; actions inconsistent with traits add stress; 4 stress levels gate mental breaks; mental break unlocks coping-mechanism traits (mostly negative). Stress 100 -> mandatory break.
relevance: Reference architecture for trait-driven narrative pressure. Maps cleanly onto bazi day-master + 用神/忌神: actions against the chart accumulate 'narrative dissonance' that eventually forces a beat.

title: Caves of Qud procedural mutation/history system
key_claim: 70+ mutation traits, mutation points spent at level-up. World seeded with 5 generated Sultans whose biographies populate ruins/lore via word-of-mouth/text artifacts (Dwarf Fortress lineage). WFC (wave-function collapse) for buildings.
relevance: Industry reference for trait-as-prior + generated history that propagates through narrative artifacts. Closest 'live-world' analogue to bazi-driven destiny propagation.

title: Disco Elysium - Thought Cabinet & 24-skill personality system
key_claim: 24 personified skills literally talk to player. Thoughts internalize over time and rewire dialogue options - merely accepting an unprocessed thought changes voice. Red/white skill checks gate narrative branches.
relevance: Industry-validated proof that interior personality machinery can be made *the* gameplay surface. Shows the upper bound of where bazi-driven internal voice could go in a novel sim.

title: Sullivan - Tarot-based Narrative Generation (FDG 2018)
key_claim: Anne Sullivan (Georgia Tech) academic paper on tarot-as-narrative-generator. Cards' rich symbolism gives a corpus for high variance story synopses + tagline generation.
relevance: Academic precedent for the exact methodological move we're proposing - using a small fixed symbolic system as a generative scaffold.

title: Mythic GME 2E + Ironsworn oracle systems
key_claim: Mythic GME: scene/event/list-driven solo RPG; Fate Chart for yes/no oracles; Action+Subject meaning tables. Ironsworn parallels with Action+Theme oracle and 'Tell the Truth' tables that establish world facts.
relevance: Battle-tested scaffolds for solo narrative generation. The Action+Subject pair is a 1-symbol-pair-> sentence-fragment generator and a perfect test bed for bazi-symbol-pair -> branch-prompt mapping.
reliability: High. Decade+ of solo-RPG community use.

title: 玄派 / 墨星写作 / 笔灵 / 星月写作 (Chinese web-novel author tools)
key_claim: Major Chinese web-novel writing assistants offer character-name generators, level/realm generators, technique-name generators, item-grade generators, plot-formula generators (200+ on 笔灵). Cultivation-genre coverage strong, but no explicit bazi/qimen/命格 module documented across 玄派/墨星/笔灵/星月写作.
relevance: Confirms our differentiation gap: nobody in the Chinese web-novel tooling market has shipped bazi-driven character/branch consistency. There is a real product opening.

title: 凡人修仙传 (Wonglu) worldbuilding consistency analysis
key_claim: Genre-defining cultivation novel praised specifically for logical self-consistency of cultivation/resource economy. 命格 not made an explicit mechanical layer in canonical text; consistency is enforced via author discipline rather than algorithm.
relevance: Reference for 'good' cultivation worldbuilding without algorithmic 命格. Shows the gap: top novels imply 命格 but don't compute it; an author tool could.

title: Cantian AI wiki - Shensha & metaphysics knowledge graph
key_claim: Shensha (神煞) = auxiliary symbolic stars: ~20+ classical entries (天乙贵人, 文昌, 桃花, 羊刃, 劫煞, ...) used as event-flavor modifiers atop the Ten Gods structural skeleton. Cantian organizes 山命通会 + 易经 etc. into knowledge-graph form for LLM consumption.
relevance: Provides the symbolic-modifier vocabulary we'd map to event archetypes (e.g. 桃花 -> romance branch weight, 羊刃 -> conflict-prone branch).
reliability: Medium-high. Commercial vendor but transparently sourced classics.

title: 真太阳时 / 早晚子时 controversies
key_claim: Three time-systems used by practitioners: 北京时间 (mean solar), 真太阳时 (true solar), 平气定气 (constant/positioned solar terms). Boundary controversy: 23:00-24:00 is 晚子时 - mainstream switches day at 23:00 but minority view defers to midnight. Affects pillar generation directly.
relevance: Critical configuration knob the project must make explicit (default + override). Honesty surface for the user.

title: PMC8226147 - Universality of Science and TCM (philosophical survey)
key_claim: Qi/TCM-style metaphysical concepts are non-falsifiable in the Popperian sense; epistemically distinct from scientific claims. Pragmatic stance: useful instruments need not be scientifically true to be functionally indispensable.
relevance: Provides the framing language for our honesty disclaimer: we use bazi as a *narrative consistency prior*, not a divinatory truth claim.

title: anthonylee1994/qimen + ownthink/Qimen + Yvainovski/QiMenDunJia
key_claim: Smaller qimen 排盘 implementations (TS/Python/Java), academic or hobby-grade. Ecosystem is fragmented; no dominant qimen lib comparable to lunar-javascript's position for bazi.
relevance: Shows we can probably stand up our own thin qimen layer over lunar-javascript primitives; no clear must-adopt dependency.

title: PANGeA - Procedural AI Narrative for Turn-Based Games (arxiv 2404.19721)
key_claim: LLM dynamically evaluates inputs against game rules; uses Big Five personality model to constrain NPC responses for narrative consistency. General pattern: symbolic-prior + LLM-generation > LLM-only.
relevance: Methodological parallel to BaZi-LLM (S5): replace Big Five with bazi 十神/格局 as a richer culturally-grounded prior.

title: 阅文作家助手 / 阅文妙笔大模型 (Yuewen Group official portal)
key_claim: Qidian/Yuewen's in-house authoring suite. Integrates DeepSeek-R1 + the proprietary 阅文妙笔 (Miaobi) web-novel LLM. Four flagship features: worldview design, character design, scene description, combat description. Used 100k+ times/week.
relevance: Direct competitor / reference. Most established CN platform-native AI tool for our target audience (Qidian xianxia authors).

title: 阅文集团率先部署DeepSeek，邀请全行业作家共同探索AI赋能创作 (BJ Daily, 2025-02-05)
key_claim: Yuewen integrated independently-deployed DeepSeek-R1 into 作家助手 in Feb 2025, upgrading three modules: smart Q&A, inspiration retrieval, prose polishing. CEO position: 'AIGC will not replace authors; it is the golden finger; the protagonist remains the author.'
relevance: Establishes that even the dominant CN platform sees AI as assistant, not generator. Validates our 'co-pilot' framing.

title: 番茄小说基于豆包大模型推出AI辅助写作 (industry summary, 2024-2025)
key_claim: Fanqie launched AI-assist in May 2024 using ByteDance's 豆包 model: 7 features—AI 扩写, AI 改写, 自定义描写, AI 续写, AI 起名, 卡文锦囊, AI 助手. Sept 2024 'AI training clause' provoked author backlash.
relevance: Fanqie is largest free CN reader platform; their AI policy directly drives 5,000-AI-novels-per-day flooding.

title: AI写作催生出番茄小说单日五千本首秀奇观 (Zhihu / multiple, March 2025)
key_claim: Post-DeepSeek (Jan 2025), Fanqie daily new-book debuts went 400 → 5,606 (1302% YoY). Platform responded April 2025 with stricter 签约 review and refusal of repeat low-quality submitters.
relevance: Shows the 'AI flood' market reality: low-quality, high-volume AI generators are the dominant deployment, not quality-focused systems.

title: 七猫中文网 AI小助理 + 文心一言 (Qimao official notice, 2023+)
key_claim: Qimao built 'AI 小助理' on Baidu 文心一言: 3 modules—码字灵感, 故事设定, 角色起名. Lighter than Yuewen Miaobi.
relevance: Mid-tier free platform AI; thin wrapper around general LLM, not novel-tuned.

title: 蛙蛙写作 (Wawa Writer, Hangzhou Yinli Zhihang)
key_claim: China's most-reviewed standalone AI novel tool. Strong on '黄金三章' opening (80% conflict-construction efficiency claim). Documented weakness: '20章后易出现战力崩坏或人设偏移'—long-novel control fails after ~20 chapters. Parent 波形智能 acquired by OPPO.
relevance: Closest commercial peer; their 20-chapter ceiling is the EXACT problem we are trying to solve for 100k+字 cultivation.

title: 笔灵AI (Biling AI)
key_claim: Vertical CN web-novel tool. '新建作品'-storage of outline+chapters lets AI 续写 recall full settings, partially solving '失忆症'. Reviewed as 'most web-novel-fluent six-sided fighter'.
relevance: Demonstrates that author-managed outline+character store is the de-facto pattern in CN tooling. Our agent+八字内核 is one layer above this.

title: 星月写作 (Xingyue Writing)
key_claim: Full-pipeline CN tool covering 灵感→大纲→正文→润色→发布. Claims 92% character-personality consistency vs Wawa 65% / GPT-4 40%. 18 web-novel sub-genre templates including 玄幻/仙侠. Auto-extracts '高频爆点元素' from Qidian/Fanqie/Feilu.
relevance: Most ambitious commercial CN system. Genre-specific tuning exists but is 套路-pattern-mining, not metaphysical-system-aware.

title: 码哩写作 (Mali Writing)
key_claim: AI-assist focused on 'no obvious AI traces' through process-driven steps: 故事设置→要素→大纲→章纲→正文. Targets continuation from any chapter.
relevance: Process-discipline approach (vs raw generation). Useful pattern for our chapter-of-the-day cadence.

title: 豆包 (ByteDance Doubao)
key_claim: Doubao model deployed across 50+ ByteDance properties incl. Fanqie. Powers Fanqie's AI generation features. General-purpose, not novel-tuned at base.
relevance: Default model behind Fanqie AI flood. Wide reach but commodity quality.

title: DeepSeek 写网文 修仙 玄幻 (CSDN industry guide, 2025)
key_claim: DeepSeek used by authors for: worldbuilding skeleton (3-min generation of 势力分布/修炼体系/核心矛盾), logic checking, contradiction detection. Explicit caveat: '不要用它来生成正文，它的梗都太老土了'—do not use it for prose, tropes feel dated.
relevance: Authors converged on DeepSeek for outline/setting management, NOT for prose. Confirms scaffolding-vs-generation split.

title: 晋江文学城 AI辅助写作使用规范 (Jinjiang official policy, 2025-02-18)
key_claim: First CN platform with formal AI policy. Allows 3 levels: 文字型辅助 (proofreading) / 创意型辅助-要素 (inspiration) / 创意型辅助-粗纲 (rough outline). Beyond that = 锁章/黄牌/永久禁榜. CEO: '人类作者参与AI辅助是饮鸩止渴'.
relevance: Hard regulatory ceiling on full AI generation in literary CN. Defensive moat for human authors retains value.

title: 国家《人工智能生成合成内容标识办法》 + 番茄小说 AI 选项 (industry consolidated)
key_claim: Cyberspace Administration regulation effective Sept 1, 2025: platforms must label AI-generated content; authors must self-declare. Fanqie added 'AI使用' checkbox Sept 23, 2025.
relevance: Compliance baseline. Our system must support author-side AI provenance declaration.

title: WebNovelBench: Placing LLM Novelists on the Web Novel Distribution (Lin et al., arXiv:2505.14818)
key_claim: Benchmark over 4,000 CN web novels evaluated on 8 narrative dimensions (rhetorical technique, sensory description richness, character balance, etc.) via LLM-as-Judge + PCA percentile. Differentiates human masters / popular novels / LLM output. 24 SOTA LLMs ranked.
relevance: Only rigorous CN web-novel eval academia I have. Useful for evaluation methodology.

title: YILING0013/AI_NovelGenerator (GitHub)
key_claim: Sequential pipeline (NOT multi-agent): setting → directory → chapter → finalize → consistency check. Vector RAG for context. 7 modules incl. state tracking, foreshadowing mgmt, semantic search, knowledge base. Author admits low maintenance bandwidth.
relevance: Most-starred CN long-novel OSS. Shows where the OSS bar is: vector-RAG + sequential prompts. Our agent loop is a step beyond.

title: RhythmicWave/NovelForge (GitHub)
key_claim: Electron + FastAPI + SQLModel. Card-based authoring + JSON-Schema-validated AI generation. Optional Neo4j knowledge graph for character/plot relations. AGPLv3 + commercial license.
relevance: Closest in 'structured cards + schema' philosophy to what we'd want for 八字命格 / 修炼境界 cards. Lacks domain logic—just storage.

title: lingfengQAQ/webnovel-writer (GitHub)
key_claim: Built on Claude Code. 3-layer Story System: source truth (.story-system/), runtime contracts, read-model projections (.webnovel/). MASTER_SETTING.json + index.db + summaries/ + memory_scratchpad.json + RAG with reranking. 37 genre templates, 200万字+ scale claim.
relevance: MOST aligned to our intended architecture (Claude Code + state-as-files + agent loop). Worth deep study; possible fork target.

title: cjyyx/AI_Gen_Novel (GitHub)
key_claim: Multi-agent system applying cognitive writing model (planning/transformation/review) inspired by RecurrentGPT. Memory compression. Acknowledges: 'Current LLMs lack sufficient capability for long-form web novel creation.'
relevance: Rare honest CN OSS that admits the unsolved problem. Matches our 'still mostly toys' thesis.

title: xindoo/ai-novel-lab (GitHub)
key_claim: Generated complete 100-chapter, 428k+字 urban-fantasy. Layered consistency: macro worldbuilding/character/structure + micro chapter outlines + scene details. AGENTS.md as 'AI constitution'. Coherence improved 73→93/100 via revision loop.
relevance: Empirical proof that long-form CN AI novels are achievable today—but only with disciplined AGENTS.md + multi-pass revision. Genre was urban-fantasy, not xianxia.

title: MaoXiaoYuZ/Long-Novel-GPT (GitHub)
key_claim: Parallel-thread architecture for unlimited-chapter generation. Stages: chapter creation, plot expansion, manuscript refinement.
relevance: Parallel-pipeline approach. Throughput-oriented; not consistency-oriented.

title: BlinkDL/AI-Writer (GitHub)
key_claim: Original RWKV-based CN-pretrained novel generator (玄幻/言情). Predates LLM era; small specialist model.
relevance: Historical baseline. Shows CN community has been building this since GPT-2 era.

title: leenbj/novel-creator-skill (GitHub)
key_claim: Smart-State pattern using file-level long-term memory. Targets 百万字级 sustained creation.
relevance: Another file-as-state pattern; converges with webnovel-writer and our planned architecture.

title: 实测用 Claude Code 写小说，意外发现了组队开挂模式 (Huxiu / Tencent News, Nov 2025)
key_claim: Claude Code finished '5 days of work' (vs human pro novelist 4-6 hr/day) in <20 min. Sub-agent dispatch (brainstorm / outline / chapter / polish / logic-check). 3-layer memory (short=current chapter / mid=auto-summary / long=outline+character+foreshadow). Successfully placed and recovered foreshadowing across 7 chapters / 21k 字.
relevance: VALIDATES Claude Code subagent + file-state architecture for 长篇 CN novel work. Direct empirical evidence.

title: 七千万用户的网文平台，打出了「反 AI」的第一枪 (ifanr/Tencent, March 2025)
key_claim: Editor heuristics for AI detection: 'flowery vocabulary, hard transitions, unstable POV, plot-line drift, no main thread.' Identifies DeepSeek-R1 specific signature: '比喻+数字堆砌, 不必要的意象, 莫名其妙的描写'.
relevance: Concrete editor-side AI tells we must avoid. Our system needs an 'AI 痕迹去除' pass.

title: 知乎 various: AI 写小说真实评价 (multiple Zhihu zhuanlan, 2024-2025)
key_claim: Author consensus: AI good at outline/lore/duplicate-checking, bad at: emotional shading, '人设崩坏', POV slips, name/place inconsistency, '套路同质化', dated tropes. Only ~10% of AI-assisted submissions get 签约. AI 'predigested-meal' (预制菜) feeling pervasive.
relevance: Author community ground truth on what works and what doesn't. Our metaphysical-grounded prose layer must beat these failure modes.

title: 修仙小说设定构造（3）境界 (Zhihu zhuanlan, ongoing)
key_claim: Cultivation 境界 system must bind to worldview + character growth-line + 心魔. 4-5 tiers optimal (e.g. 凡人 9-tier: 炼气-筑基-结丹-元婴-化神-炼虚-合体-大乘-渡劫). Tier inflation + power-creep is the #1 failure of mediocre authors AND of AI generators.
relevance: Domain knowledge AI lacks structurally. 境界 progression is non-trivial state machine that requires explicit modeling.

title: DeepSeek成「算命大师」，AI的尽头是玄学？ (TMT Post, 2025)
key_claim: DeepSeek used heavily for 八字/玄学 'fortune-telling'. Reader complaint: 'inputting different birth-data still produces near-identical outputs'—LLM lacks real metaphysical engine, only does plausible-style imitation.
relevance: CRITICAL. LLM 算命 is statistical mimicry, not 八字奇门 logic. This is exactly the void our project's 'metaphysical kernel' fills.

title: 中国一通百通：八字、奇门、太乙、紫薇、梅花、六爻、六壬、河洛 (Zhihu zhuanlan)
key_claim: Survey of major CN metaphysical systems and their interconnection (天干地支 / 阴阳五行 base). Most can be encoded as deterministic state machines.
relevance: Our 八字奇门 internal kernel can use deterministic CN metaphysics rules — provably sound — as ground truth grounding.

title: hestudy/snowflake-fiction (GitHub)
key_claim: Snowflake-method workflow with command tree for 短篇 1-3万字 / 长篇 10-50万字 / 巨著 100万+字. CN-localized adaptation of Randy Ingermanson method.
relevance: Outline-discipline workflow. Compatible plug-in for our system.

title: ExplosiveCoderflome/AI-Novel-Writing-Assistant (GitHub)
key_claim: AI-Native long-novel system: Agent + worldview + 写法引擎 + RAG + 整本生产工作流. Targets idea→full novel automation.
relevance: Most architecturally similar to a serious 'agent platform for novelists' design. Worth code review.

title: AI落地的苦，没有人比网文圈更懂 (36Kr, 2025-03)
key_claim: Verdict from CN web-novel industry insiders: AI tools are commercially live but practically struggle on tone, voice, character logic. Editors can identify AI prose by signature failure modes; flooding crashed quality bar.
relevance: Best single CN industry-side reality check on AI novel deployment.

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
authors: Joon Sung Park, Joseph C. O'Brien, Carrie J. Cai, Meredith Ringel Morris, Percy Liang, Michael S. Bernstein
key_claim: Memory stream + reflection + planning architecture produces believable human behavior in 25-agent Smallville sandbox over 2 sim-days; ablations show all three components are necessary for believability.
relevance: Foundational reference architecture. Defines memory_stream, recency/importance/relevance retrieval, hierarchical planning, and reflection-trees that became the de-facto pattern.

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (ACM Full Text)
key_claim: Agents wake, plan, converse, reflect; 25 agents lived in Smallville for 2 sim-days; Valentine party emerged as multi-day social-coordination evaluation.
relevance: Source-of-truth canonical formulation. Limits surfaced: hallucination, memory-retrieval failures, formal-LLM tone bleed, fragile importance scoring outside everyday domains.

title: joonspk-research/generative_agents
key_claim: Official Python implementation of Smallville, Apache 2.0 licensed.
relevance: Reference implementation. Pyhton + PyGame, GPT-3.5/4 backed. Useful for prompts, retrieval scoring, reflection tree code.

title: Generative Agent Simulations of 1,000 People
authors: Joon Sung Park, Carolyn Q. Zou, Aaron Shaw, Benjamin Mako Hill, Michael S. Bernstein, Percy Liang, et al.
key_claim: Agents grounded in 2-hour interview transcripts replicate held-out GSS responses at 86% of human test-retest reliability vs. 74% for demographic-only baseline.
relevance: Park's own follow-up. Pivots from 'simulated town' to 'simulated individual': interview-grounded agent. Implies our world should ground each character via deep persona doc, not bullet-list traits.

title: StanfordHCI/genagents (1000-people repo)
key_claim: Public agent bank with restricted-access individualized responses for academic users.
relevance: Pattern for persona-anchored agents; useful prompt templates.

title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
authors: Guanzhi Wang et al. (NVIDIA, Caltech, UT Austin, Stanford)
key_claim: Auto-curriculum + skill library (executable code) + iterative self-verification yields 3.3x more unique items, 15.3x faster tech-tree progress vs. prior SOTA in Minecraft.
relevance: Skill-library is the procedural-memory analogue we want for characters: cooking, swordsmanship, ledger-keeping become reusable callable functions. Iterative-prompt loop is a model for 'agent retries action when feedback shows failure'.

title: Voyager Project Page
key_claim: Lifelong learning agent with growing JS skill library, GPT-4 prompted black-box.
relevance: Demonstrates that without fine-tuning, prompt + code-as-skill scales further than monolithic prompts.

title: Project Sid: Many-agent simulations toward AI civilization
authors: Altera AI (Robert G. Yang et al.)
key_claim: PIANO architecture runs 50-1000+ agents in Minecraft. Emergent specialization, constitution amendment, religion spread (Pastafarianism), cultural-meme transmission across 500-agent run.
relevance: Existence proof that >25-agent coherent civilization is achievable in 2024; PIANO splits cognition into ~10 parallel modules vs. Park's serial pipeline.

title: altera-al/project-sid
key_claim: Tech report + code for PIANO architecture (~10 modules: action, planning, social-awareness, action-awareness, speak, etc.).
relevance: Reference for parallel-module agent design with central-decision module that aggregates parallel deliberations.

title: A Minecraft town of AI characters made friends, invented jobs, and spread religion (MIT Technology Review)
key_claim: Independent reporting on Project Sid emergent behaviors at 500-agent scale; cultural-meme spread documented.
relevance: Validates academic claims, including that scale alone unlocks new emergence.

title: AgentSociety: Large-Scale Simulation of LLM-Driven Generative Agents
key_claim: MQTT-based distributed engine sustains >10,000 agents and 5M agent-environment interactions on cluster; >10^6 agent-steps/hour.
relevance: Engineering reference for distributed agent simulation; uses MQTT message bus, cognitive substrate (needs/emotions/motivation). Open source on GitHub.

title: tsinghua-fib-lab/AgentSociety
key_claim: Distributed simulation runtime open-sourced.
relevance: Heaviest production-grade agent-society code base in 2025.

title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
key_claim: Hierarchical OS-like memory management: main context + scratch + recall + archival; tools to swap pages in/out via function calls.
relevance: OS-style memory tiering. Best-known production-ish pattern for indefinite conversational agents. Now Letta.

title: A-Mem: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents (NeurIPS 2025)
authors: Wujiang Xu, Zujie Liang, Kai Mei, Hang Gao, Juntao Tan, Yongfeng Zhang
key_claim: Zettelkasten-inspired notes with auto-linking and dynamic re-organization; outperforms vector-only baselines on six foundation models.
relevance: More structured than memory_stream: notes have keywords, tags, links forming a knowledge graph that grows over time.

title: agiresearch/A-mem
key_claim: Reference implementation for A-Mem.
relevance: Direct adoption candidate for character-memory.

title: Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory
key_claim: Hybrid vector+graph+kv store; +26% LLM-as-judge over OpenAI memory, -91% p95 latency, -90% token cost. Graph variant +2pp accuracy at +1.15s p95.
relevance: Production-grade memory stack with concrete cost/latency numbers; library used by 100k+ devs.

title: mem0ai/mem0
key_claim: Universal memory layer; Kuzu embedded graph + vector DB.
relevance: Drop-in for character memory; Kuzu means no separate DB server.

title: State of AI Agent Memory 2026 (Mem0)
key_claim: Graph memory moved from experimental to production in 2025; recommends hybrid vector+graph for relationship-heavy domains.
relevance: Justifies graph-memory for our character-relationship-heavy simulation.

title: Memory in the Age of AI Agents (survey, Dec 2025)
key_claim: Decomposes agent memory into episodic (events), semantic (de-contextualized facts), procedural (reusable skills); domain-specific weightings: game agents need episodic+procedural; assistants need semantic.
relevance: Framework for what memory types our character agents need: episodic (their day) + semantic (world lore) + procedural (their skills/habits).

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
key_claim: Actor + Evaluator + Self-Reflection trio; verbal RL stored as text in episodic buffer improves task success across coding, decision, reasoning benchmarks.
relevance: Reflection loop blueprint. Caveat: same model as actor and judge creates conflict-of-interest.

title: Self-Reflection in LLM Agents: Effects on Problem-Solving Performance
key_claim: Reflection helps when grounded in external feedback (citations, code execution, search) but degenerates into 'overthinking' when ungrounded; reflection raises first-answer correctness more than self-correction.
relevance: Tells us when reflection helps vs. hurts: ground every reflection in external memory or world-state delta, not free-form rumination.

title: Reflection Agents (LangChain Blog)
key_claim: ReAct, Reflexion, Self-Refine, ToT, CRITIC are stratified by external-feedback dependency; CRITIC + tools is most reliable for production.
relevance: Practical taxonomy of reflection patterns + LangGraph implementations.

title: QSAF: A Mitigation Framework for Cognitive Degradation in Agentic AI
key_claim: Defines 'cognitive degradation' as progressive breakdown of reasoning, memory retrieval, plan coherence due to token overload, planner recursion, memory starvation, context drift; 28% of ReAct paths show latent plan decay leading to goal abandonment.
relevance: Empirical evidence that long-running agents drift; gives explicit failure modes to monitor (context-drift, planner-recursion). Argues for a watchdog process.

title: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen vs OpenAgents (2026)
key_claim: LangGraph won enterprise share by 2026 due to graph state, audit trails, persistent checkpoints; CrewAI is fastest to ship for role-based crews; AutoGen is conversational; MetaGPT specialized to SDLC.
relevance: Picks: LangGraph for orchestration backbone, CrewAI when narrative needs role-team metaphor, AutoGen if free-form character chat is dominant.

title: LangGraph Persistence (LangChain Docs)
key_claim: PostgresSaver checkpointer required in production for long-running graphs; survives crashes; supports interrupt/resume; pending-writes preserved per super-step.
relevance: Critical primitive for 'tick forever' world: checkpoint each tick to Postgres so process restarts don't lose state.

title: OpenAI Swarm (now Agents SDK)
key_claim: Stateless handoff-based multi-agent primitive; superseded by OpenAI Agents SDK in 2025 for production.
relevance: Lightweight pattern but stateless; not appropriate alone for 'always on' world. Useful for transient sub-agents within ticks.

title: a16z-infra/ai-town
key_claim: MIT-licensed deployable starter kit for AI town; TypeScript + Convex backend; supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM and llama3+mxbai-embed-large by default.
relevance: Closest open scaffold to what we want. TS stack matches our codebase. Convex provides the persistent simulation engine, transactions, scheduled jobs (i.e., ticks).

title: ai-town/ARCHITECTURE.md
key_claim: Convex shared global state, transactions, simulation engine designed for multi-player; characters configurable in characters.ts.
relevance: Concrete reference for tick loop, agent state machine, embedding-based memory recall.

title: SillyTavern World Info docs
key_claim: Lorebook = keyword-triggered prompt-injection database; bound to character, persona, or chat; can carry behavioral directives, not just lore.
relevance: Battle-tested 'world info' pattern from RP community for keeping context lean while injecting only-needed lore. Useful pattern even if we don't adopt SillyTavern proper.

title: SillyTavern-MemoryBooks
key_claim: Auto-summarizes chats into structured lorebook entries.
relevance: Pattern for generating reflection notes that become injectable world-info.

title: Thytu/Agentarium
key_claim: Open-source framework for AI-agent simulations and emergent environments.
relevance: Smaller alternative to AgentSociety; lighter stack.

title: Constella: Supporting Storywriters' Interconnected Character Creation through LLM-based Multi-Agents
key_claim: Multi-agent system for parallel character development; writer-defined personas converse and surface inter-character tensions.
relevance: Closest published academic system to our use case (story-writing aided by agent personas).

title: NovelAI V4/V4.5 Multi-Character Workflow (2026)
key_claim: Trait isolation per character via separate conditioning streams to prevent token competition.
relevance: Production technique for multi-character coherence.

title: Can A Society of Generative Agents Simulate Human Behavior and Inform Public Health Policy?
key_claim: Generative-agent society used for vaccine-hesitancy simulation; fragile when worldview diverges from training distribution.
relevance: Empirical case-study confirming Park's importance-scoring caveat: outside everyday life, the heuristics break.

title: Validation is the central challenge for generative social simulation
key_claim: Critical review of LLM-based ABMs concludes that validation, not capability, is the bottleneck for trustworthy multi-day simulations.
relevance: Directly supports our concern about evaluating long-running coherence. Recommends triangulation: micro-behavior fidelity + macro-pattern reproduction + author-judgment.

title: Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 pricing
key_claim: Haiku 4.5 at $1.00/$5.00 per MTok; GPT-4.1 mini at $0.40/$1.60; Batch API gives 50% discount on Anthropic and OpenAI.
relevance: Concrete 2026 unit economics for cheap-tier always-on agents. ~10x cheaper than Park 2023 GPT-4 prices.

title: LLM API Pricing Comparison 2025 (Intuition Labs)
key_claim: LLM API prices dropped roughly 10x in two years; GPT-4 class went from $30/MTok input to $2-3/MTok.
relevance: Validates that cost-feasibility for always-on sims is dramatically better in 2026 vs. 2023.

title: Dwarf Fortress (Wikipedia overview)
key_claim: DF generates a fantasy world via 1000-year procedural history step where civilizations interact, then preserves that world as the substrate for Fortress and Adventurer modes. Two-decade development; gold standard of agent-driven historical simulation.
relevance: Core reference for world-gen->legends->play pipeline architecture. Our 'tick world state continuously' is the same paradigm.

title: Bay 12 Games dev story / Threetoe stories
key_claim: Zach 'Threetoe' Adams writes short fiction that he and Tarn analyse to extract simulation primitives. Stories drive feature design rather than scripts.
relevance: Anti-pattern check: do not over-script. Direct evidence that story->mechanic extraction works long-term.

title: ThreeToe page (Dwarf Fortress Wiki)
key_claim: Threetoe's stories are explicitly 'used to drive future development' via post-story analysis listings.
relevance: Confirms the fiction-first design loop.

title: DF2014 Legends mode (DF Wiki)
key_claim: Legends mode exposes the simulation as queryable history: characters, civs, conflicts, artifacts, books-of-books recorded as causal sentences.
relevance: Direct analog to our 'canon stages' - narrative is a side-effect of simulation, not the simulation.

title: Procedural Humanities: Interview with Tarn Adams (HASTAC, 2010)
key_claim: Adams' design vision: 'we don't want another cheap fantasy universe - we want a cheap fantasy universe generator.' Roadmap targets emergent narrative + procedural culture as terminal goal.
relevance: Philosophical anchor: distinguishes simulator-as-substrate vs simulator-as-author.

title: Recurse Games: Generating Procedural Myths and Histories
key_claim: Adams' myth generator seeds with one entity (god/cosmic egg) and grows mythology by random spawning + interaction events; mythology is a list of (entity,interaction) tuples.
relevance: Direct template for Bazi-cosmology layer: seed deterministic priors, then step.

title: Tarn Adams on simulating magic systems (PC Gamer)
key_claim: Adams targets a magic system with deeper simulation than any existing system; cosmology is procedurally generated per world.
relevance: Per-world cosmology is also our target (Bazi/Qimen are per-protagonist priors).

title: RimWorld AI Storytellers (RimWorld Wiki)
key_claim: Three storyteller personalities - Cassandra (rising tension), Phoebe (chillax/peace), Randy (chaos) - drive event scheduling. The storyteller is a meta-narrative agent on top of simulation.
relevance: Direct match for our 'consistency-gate scoring' + author-decision-needed pause: the storyteller is the gating layer.

title: The Story Generator: Game Design Analysis of RimWorld (Substack)
key_claim: Sylvester (designer) structures RW as a 'story generator' - simulation feeds storyteller, storyteller arms triggers, output is unique-to-playthrough drama.
relevance: Architectural pattern: separate sim from drama-manager from output renderer.

title: RimWorld vs DF: procedurally generated storytelling (Game Developer)
key_claim: DF is bottom-up emergence; RW is top-down storyteller-curated emergence. Both produce 'stories' but RW is more legible.
relevance: Critical decision point: pure emergence (DF) vs author-aware curation (RW).

title: CK3 Traits (Paradox Wiki)
key_claim: Trait system: congenital, personality, education, lifestyle. Congenital traits inheritable with leveling chains (Attractiveness/Intelligence/Strength six-tier ladders).
relevance: Reference architecture for 'Bazi as inherited prior + acquired modifiers' character model.

title: CK3 Stress / Mental Break (Paradox Wiki)
key_claim: Stress accumulates when actions go against trait nature; mental breaks fire trait-specific events (wrathful->yelling at L1, murdering heir at L3).
relevance: Tension-budget mechanic for forcing dramatic events. Excellent prior-violation cost model.

title: Like Sands Through The Hour Glass: CK3 Emergent Narrative (Paste)
key_claim: CK3's procedural narrative is an asset because of its uncontrollability; combinatorial trait permutations make every dynasty feel novelistic.
relevance: Validation: trait-density + small randomness = novel-grade narrative.

title: Dev Diary #31: A Stressful Situation (Paradox Forum)
key_claim: Designers explicitly describe stress as the bridge between trait personality and gameplay; 'forcing characters to act against nature' is the core dramatic primitive.
relevance: Steal: explicit prior-violation accounting.

title: Subverting Historical Cause & Effect: Mythic Biographies in Caves of Qud (Grinblat & Bucklew, FDG'17)
key_claim: Sultan biographies: state-machine drives random sultan actions, then a 'sifting' pattern infers post-hoc motivations. 'History is what you remember, biased by retelling.'
relevance: CRUCIAL: post-hoc motivation inference is the right mental model for retroactively rationalizing simulator output into novel-grade text.

title: Caves of Qud (Wikipedia)
key_claim: Sultans + stratified ruins + Qudpedia: each playthrough has a five-Sultan history with conflicting accounts and parchment fragments hidden in dungeons.
relevance: Multi-source canon = 'each NPC's truth differs from absolute history.' Match for our consistency gate that allows POV variants.

title: Math for Game Devs: End-to-End Procedural Generation in Caves of Qud (GDC Vault)
key_claim: Bucklew explains the pipeline: world->history->civilizations->artifacts->dungeons->lore-fragments, all procedural. Each layer constrains the next.
relevance: Layered procedural pipeline pattern.

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (Park et al, UIST'23)
key_claim: 25-agent Smallville with memory-stream + reflection + planning. Agents form opinions, plan days, gossip, throw a Valentine's Day party emergently.
relevance: Foundational LLM-agent architecture. Memory stream + retrieval + reflection IS the canonical pattern we need.

title: Stanford HAI: Computational Agents Exhibit Believable Humanlike Behavior
key_claim: Ablation shows all three components (memory, reflection, planning) needed; removing any degrades believability sharply.
relevance: Don't shortcut: all three layers are load-bearing.

title: Generative Agents repository (GitHub joonspk-research)
key_claim: Open-source Smallville code: scheduler, perception, retrieval, prompt templates, sandbox.
relevance: Direct reference implementation we can fork/study.

title: Park et al. dissertation: 1000-agent simulation of US adults
key_claim: Park's dissertation extends to 1000-agent population sim of US adults; documents alignment failure modes at scale.
relevance: Scaling beyond 25 is doable but requires fidelity instrumentation.

title: Project Sid: Many-agent simulations toward AI civilization (Altera, arxiv 2411.00114)
key_claim: PIANO architecture: parallel info aggregation. Up to 1000 agents in Minecraft self-organize into roles, governance, religion, taxation, cultural memes.
relevance: Demonstrates large-scale multi-agent civilization is feasible. Our character-graph approach must scale similarly when protagonist meets 100+ NPCs.

title: Project Sid GitHub (altera-al)
key_claim: Open-source Sid release including agent code, memory primitives.
relevance: Reference implementation for many-agent.

title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent (Wang et al, arxiv 2305.16291)
key_claim: GPT-4 Minecraft agent with three components: automatic curriculum + skill library (executable code) + iterative self-verification prompting. 3.3x more items, 15.3x faster tech-tree.
relevance: Skill library = re-usable narrative capabilities. We can store 'narrative moves' similarly.

title: Agents: Open-source Framework for Autonomous Language Agents (Zhou et al, arxiv 2309.07870)
key_claim: Modular framework: planning, memory, tool use, multi-agent comm, fine-grained symbolic control. Designed for non-specialists.
relevance: Off-the-shelf scaffolding option.

title: AutoGen: Multi-Agent Conversation Framework (Wu et al, arxiv 2308.08155)
key_claim: Conversational multi-agent framework. v0.4 is async/event-driven. Now in maintenance mode in favor of MS Agent Framework.
relevance: Reference for async event-driven multi-agent infrastructure.

title: MetaAgents: LLM agents for decision-making on teaming (arxiv 2310.06500)
key_claim: Job-fair simulation: LLM agents form teams via dialogue + role alignment. Performance degrades with participant count due to misalignment.
relevance: Cautionary: persona-role alignment is fragile at scale.

title: Facade: Fully-Realized Interactive Drama (Mateas & Stern, GDC'03)
key_claim: Drama manager sequences hand-authored 'beats' in response to player input. Joint dialog behaviors written in ABL reactive-planning language.
relevance: Beat-based drama-management is the canonical alternative to free emergence.

title: Structuring Content in the Facade Architecture (Mateas, AIIDE'05)
key_claim: Beats are story-atoms with preconditions, behaviors, effects on dramatic tension; drama manager queries beats and selects via tension curve.
relevance: Steal: tension-budget gates = consistency-gate.

title: Versu - A Simulationist Storytelling System (Evans & Short, 2014)
key_claim: Versu: autonomous social agents + 'social practices' (norms) + storylets. Players form unscripted relationships because the social model is generative.
relevance: Direct template for protagonist-centered relationship graph that supports unscripted bonds.

title: Introducing Versu (Emily Short blog)
key_claim: Short documents the design philosophy: 'because the social model is generative, you can have romance the author never wrote.'
relevance: Crystallizes: social-physics > scripted scenes.

title: Ink: inkle's narrative scripting language
key_claim: Ink: declarative scripting for branching narrative + variable state + knot-stitch-divert primitives + external function calls.
relevance: Industry-standard DSL for narrative; our author-decision system could compile to ink-like form.

title: Heaven's Vault: how Inkle wrote its own herstory (MCV/DEVELOP)
key_claim: Heaven's Vault uses 'narrative tweening': mix-and-match conversational atoms fill gaps between keyframes. Camera/scene driven by ink.
relevance: Tweening pattern for our 'canon stage' boundary text.

title: Mythic Game Master Emulator (Word Mill Games)
key_claim: Fate Chart (13x13) maps 'likelihood' x 'chaos factor' to yes/no/exceptional with random events; Meaning Tables (47 in 2nd ed) supply narrative detail.
relevance: Direct analog: Bazi/Qimen as our Fate Chart. The chaos-factor knob is the master tension dial.

title: Free Oracle: Mythic Fate Chart v1 (Fantasy Grounds blog)
key_claim: Detailed Fate Chart layout: rows=action rank, cols=difficulty, cells=percent chance. Chaos modifies all rolls per scene.
relevance: Probability-table-as-prior reference.

title: Ironsworn Oracles (Tomkin Press)
key_claim: 'Ask the Oracle' move: binary 'likely/unlikely' table + open-ended inspiration tables. Oracle answers feed into narrative as facts.
relevance: Lean oracle pattern; cleaner than Mythic; Bazi/Qimen produce richer answers.

title: Tarot-based narrative generation (Sullivan, FDG'18)
key_claim: Tarot-card draws -> templated movie-synopsis generator. Symbolic prior + NLP rendering.
relevance: Direct precedent for divination-prior-driven narrative gen.

title: Tarot as Procedural Storytelling (Manning, in 'Procedural Storytelling in Game Design')
key_claim: Tarot is a 'compressed dictionary of human archetypes' - random draw is a controllable randomness source for storytelling.
relevance: Cosmological priors as compression of narrative archetypes - same principle as Bazi.

title: Procedural Storytelling in Game Design (Short & Adams, eds, 2019)
key_claim: 33-essay anthology: characterization, world-building, theme, momentum from generative perspective. Edited by Tanya Short and Tarn Adams.
relevance: Master canonical reference for the field.

title: Pendragon (TTRPG, Wikipedia)
key_claim: Pendragon's Winter Phase: between adventures, characters age, marry, raise heirs, manage estates; 82-year campaign requires generational succession.
relevance: Direct template for 'months of runtime' world-tick where most ticks are non-dramatic state evolution.

title: Great Pendragon Campaign (Chaosium)
key_claim: 432-page campaign book: detailed year-by-year events 485-end-of-Arthur. Demonstrates how a fixed historical skeleton plus character agency produces 30-year campaigns.
relevance: Steal: historical-skeleton + per-character improvisation.

title: Burning Wheel: Beliefs and Instincts (publisher page)
key_claim: BITs system: Belief = strategic goal, Instinct = automatic behavior, Trait = identity descriptor. Artha (Fate/Persona/Deeds) rewards engaging with BITs.
relevance: Steal: Belief = trigger that obligates GM/sim to test it. Direct mechanism for 'obligated dramatic events'.

title: Four Pillars of Destiny (Wikipedia)
key_claim: BaZi maps year/month/day/hour to 8 stem-branch chars via 60-year sexagenary cycle (Han dynasty origin); modern Ziping method since Song dynasty.
relevance: Deterministic algorithm spec.

title: BaZi-Based Character Simulation Benchmark (Zheng et al, arxiv 2510.23337)
key_claim: First QA dataset for BaZi persona reasoning. BaZi-LLM system integrates symbolic reasoning + LLM. 30.3-62.6% accuracy gain over DeepSeek-v3/GPT-5-mini. Wrong BaZi -> 20-45% accuracy drop, proving the symbolic prior is load-bearing.
relevance: DEFINITIVE evidence that BaZi-as-prior actually improves character simulation in LLM. Direct citation for our architecture.

title: BaziQA-Benchmark: Symbolic + Temporal Reasoning in LLMs (arxiv 2602.12889)
key_claim: Companion benchmark evaluating LLMs on symbolic-and-temporally-compositional BaZi reasoning.
relevance: Eval harness reference.

title: Cantian AI / bazi-mcp (GitHub)
key_claim: MCP server providing deterministic BaZi computation (handles DST, true solar time, lunar-solar conversion).
relevance: Reference implementation we can adopt as the BaZi calculation backbone.

title: Qimen Dunjia (Wikipedia)
key_claim: 9-palace chart with 9 stars / 8 gates / 8 gods / 10 stems mapping per 2-hour interval. Originally military strategy / statecraft system.
relevance: Algorithm spec for Qimen layer.

title: QiAdvisor / Master Sean Chan / Joey Yap calculators
key_claim: Multiple deterministic calculator implementations exist; algorithm fully specified.
relevance: Confirms algorithmic tractability.

title: SCORE: Story Coherence and Retrieval Enhancement for AI Narratives (arxiv 2503.23512)
key_claim: Hybrid: dynamic state tracking + context-aware summarization + TF-IDF + semantic embedding retrieval. +23.6% coherence (NCI-2.0), 89.7% emotional consistency (EASM), -41.8% hallucinations vs baseline GPT.
relevance: Direct numerical baseline for consistency-gate metrics.

title: Lost in Stories: Consistency Bugs in Long Story Generation (arxiv 2603.05890)
key_claim: ConStory-Bench: 5 error categories, 19 subtypes. Quantifies consistency degradation as length scales.
relevance: Eval benchmark for our consistency gate.

title: Can LLMs Generate Good Stories? Narrative Planning Perspective (arxiv 2506.10161)
key_claim: LLMs fail at long-horizon narrative planning without explicit goal/state tracking. Plot-skeleton outperforms free generation.
relevance: Argues for our skeleton + tick + render architecture.

title: SNAP: Plan-Driven Framework for Controllable Interactive Narrative (arxiv 2601.11529)
key_claim: Confines context within 'Cells' with explicit plans (spatiotemporal setting + character action + plot dev), preventing drift.
relevance: Cell-based context-management pattern; matches our 'canon stage' chunking.

title: Patchview: LLM-Powered Worldbuilding with Generative Dust (arxiv 2408.04112)
key_claim: Visual+LLM worldbuilding tool with 'dust' (atomic facts) and 'magnets' (organizing concepts). Designed for author co-creation.
relevance: UI pattern for author-decision loop.

title: Drama Llama: LLM-Powered Storylets Framework (arxiv 2501.09099)
key_claim: Storylets with natural-language triggers fired by LLM. Authors define triggers in NL, no DSL programming needed.
relevance: Steal: NL-trigger storylets layer above simulation.

title: Open-Theatre: Open-Source Toolkit for LLM-based Interactive Drama (arxiv 2509.16713)
key_claim: Director-Global-Actor framework for LLM drama. Director schedules, GlobalActor maintains world consistency, Actors play roles.
relevance: Direct architectural blueprint for our system.

title: what-if: Branching Narratives via Meta-Prompting LLMs (arxiv 2412.10582)
key_claim: Zero-shot meta-prompting generates coherent branches at major plot decision points from a linear prewritten story.
relevance: Direct method for our 'branch alternatives' generation.

title: A Survey on LLMs for Story Generation (Findings of EMNLP 2025)
key_claim: Comprehensive 2025 survey of LLM story-gen field: planning, controllability, consistency, evaluation. Identifies long-form fiction as a major open frontier.
relevance: Field-level overview confirming fiction-gen+sim is still green field.

title: Beyond the Context Window: Architecting Long-Form Story Generation (Pratilipi engineering blog, Feb 2026)
key_claim: 'Long-form generation isn't a prompting problem; it's a systems engineering problem.' Stories as stateful systems with constraints, contracts, memory.
relevance: Industry validation: our architectural framing is correct.

title: Book-Agent: Epistemically Controlled Long-Form Fiction Pipeline (Level1Techs)
key_claim: Maintains per-character knowledge state across timelines; emotional arcs, parallel plot threads.
relevance: Hobbyist confirmation of multi-character POV bookkeeping.

title: NovelAI Lorebook architecture documentation
key_claim: Lorebook: dynamic context injection of worldbuilding entries based on text triggers; Story Bible (Sudowrite) is parallel concept.
relevance: Industry pattern for selective context injection - matches our 'character set is dynamic' need.

title: Character.AI PipSqueak 2 + Lorebook infrastructure shift
key_claim: Character.AI rolling out persistent memory + lorebook to address character drift; explicit acknowledgment that long-context alone is insufficient.
relevance: Industry-scale validation of memory+lorebook pattern.
